_comment: sovereign-gatewayd dashboard. Visualizes the daemon's own GET /metrics namespace (sovereign_gateway_*, served directly over HTTP — no textfile collector; add a scrape job for the daemon's bind address, default 127.0.0.1:8787). Pairs with the 2 alert rules (SovereignGatewayCloudSpill / SovereignGatewayTripwireUnmonitored) in config/prometheus/alerts/sovereign-gatewayd.rules.yml and the runbook sections in docs/operator/m060-deployment-guide.md. The headline panel is the never-cloud-spill sovereignty tripwire: the daemon forces allow_cloud=false on every request, so the holds-gauge dropping to 0 is a boundary failure, never tuning. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model.
title: sovereign-os — gateway daemon (never-cloud-spill)

panels.title: never-cloud-spill invariant
panels.description: The sovereignty tripwire. The daemon forces allow_cloud=false on every request before routing; 1 = no decision has ever routed to the cloud plane in this process's lifetime. 0 fires SovereignGatewayCloudSpill (critical) — treat as an incident, see the runbook.

panels.title: cloud spills (process lifetime)
panels.description: Decisions that routed to the cloud plane. Must stay 0 — any non-zero value is the same incident as the tripwire breaking (the gauge is exactly spills == 0).

panels.title: live gateway surfaces
panels.description: Gateway contract surfaces currently Live (of the 6 in the M048 Module-4 manifest, GET /manifest).

panels.title: requests handled (lifetime)
panels.description: Inference requests handled since the daemon started (infer + simple-infer; read-only explain/deliberate count separately as dry-runs).

panels.title: request + dry-run rate (5m)
panels.description: Decision traffic vs read-only auditing traffic. dry-runs are /v1/explain + /v1/deliberate — they never learn or touch the ledger.

panels.title: decisions by disposition (5m rate)
panels.description: committed = ran + admitted into Memory-OS · refused = engine declined (422 to the client) · learned = admitted into recall. A rising refused share means requests are arriving that the value-plane or scheduler won't accept.

panels.title: routing per SRP role (5m rate)
panels.description: Which SRP role each committed decision routed to (conductor / scout / oracle / …). The shape of this chart IS the router's live policy — a drift here without a config change is worth investigating.

panels.title: world-model prior agreement
panels.description: Of the decisions that carried an M030 World-Model prior, the fraction whose prior agreed with the live verdict — how well the engine is learning its own routing-outcome dynamics over the process lifetime. Climbs as Memory-OS accumulates (topic, role) → outcome evidence.

options.content: **never-cloud-spill** — the daemon is local-first by construction (`force_local`): every request is forced `allow_cloud = false` before it reaches the router, so a client can never push work off-node. The tripwire gauge above is the process-lifetime witness of that invariant; `config/prometheus/alerts/sovereign-gatewayd.rules.yml` pages on it breaking (critical) and on it going unobserved (`absent()`, warning). Runbooks: deployment guide § *sovereign-gatewayd alerts*. Gateway doctrine (from `sovereign-gateway`): *"Instead of tools owning provider keys: client → Sovereign Gateway → local/cloud/model router"*.
